content.purpose: Test locality-sensitive hashing as an alternative to cosine top-k for readout.
content.method: Hash codebook vectors and inputs using random hyperplane LSH. Count per-class hash bucket collisions as votes.
content.result: Functional but does not beat top-k cosine. LSH trades exact similarity for speed, which is not the current bottleneck at codebook sizes ~10K.
content.key_insight: At current codebook sizes, exact cosine top-k is fast enough. LSH becomes relevant at 100K+ vectors.
